text: Good API design follows several key practices. REST APIs should have clear, logical endpoints that represent resources, like `/users` or `/orders`. Each endpoint should use the correct HTTP method — GET for fetching data, POST for creating, and DELETE for removing. Use standard status codes, such as 200 for success, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Always include documentation that explains how to use the API, including examples. Finally, use versioning in your URLs, like `/v1/users`, so changes don't break existing applications. Good design makes APIs easy to understand and maintain.
